headline: Inflation seen peaking in May, energy dragging June lower
detail: Kalshi traders pricing in a June inflation dip driven by falling energy prices. Lower inflation prints reduce pressure on the Fed to stay restrictive, which is historically a direct lift for high-multiple tech and chip names. This supports your QQQ, SPY, CRWD, and SMH positions.
rec: Nothing to force today, but this backdrop makes your existing CRWD and QQQ HOLDs easier to sit in. If CRWD dips toward the scanner's 773.99-774.76 entry zone with stop 769.28, that's a clean add level worth watching - you already own it.

headline: CoreWeave and Nebius tumble as Meta threatens cloud
detail: Meta positioning as a fresh cloud competitor hits AI-infrastructure names hard. This is directly relevant to your chip and semiconductor exposure - SOXL, SMH, and anything riding the AI buildout narrative could see spillover selling. It adds another reason to stay cautious on leveraged chip positions.
rec: No new action, but this headline reinforces trimming SOXL on any bounce rather than adding. Watch SMH for follow-through weakness given your -1.71% position there.

headline: Nvidia betting on trillion-dollar robotics boom
detail: Nvidia's robotics push is a longer-term catalyst for the semiconductor ecosystem broadly - AMAT, ASML, KLAC, and SMH all benefit indirectly as chip demand expands beyond AI servers. No immediate trade trigger but it underpins the bull case for your chip names if macro cooperates.
rec: No action today - this is a thesis validator, not a catalyst. KLAC has the cleanest scanner setup (7/12, R 18.7) in the chip space if you want robotics exposure, but wait for macro clarity before adding margin risk.

headline: U.S. won't renew USMCA, opens Canada/Mexico negotiations
detail: Trade policy uncertainty is back on the table. Supply chains for semiconductors and tech hardware have cross-border dependencies; renewed tariff risk adds a macro overhang to chip names specifically. On margin, macro surprises like this are the kind of gap-down risk that stops don't always catch.
rec: No trade here, but a reminder to keep stops honest on your leveraged names SOXL and SPAL - this kind of headline can gap things overnight and margin won't protect you if price opens through your stop.
